item.aerialhell.smithing_template.arsoni: Helyezz ide Netherit páncélt, fegyvert, vagy eszközt

item.aerialhell.shadow_armor.disabled_re: Figyelem: A textúrák lassan frissülnek a chunk frissítésekkel.

ability.aerialhell.ninja.desc_2: Rövid ideig Láthatatlanságot és Gyorsaságot biztosít.
ability.aerialhell.ninja_master.desc_2: Rövid ideig Láthatatlanságot és Gyorsaság II hatást biztosít.
ability.aerialhell.random_sword.desc: Képesség: Véletlenszerű hatás a következők közül:
ability.aerialhell.antidote_sword.desc: Képesség: Megszünteti a Mérgezés és Sorvadás hatásokat.
ability.aerialhell.netherian_king.desc: x2 sebzés az Alvilágban. Képesség: Tűzállóság.
ability.aerialhell.netherian_king.desc_2: Találat esetén: Lángba borítja a célpontot.
ability.aerialhell.netherian_king.condit: Aktív képesség használatának feltétele: Nem lehet vízben, vagy esőben lenni.
ability.aerialhell.netherian_king.cooldo: Hátralévő idő: 30mp (15mp, ha fel van szerelve teljes Arsonist Páncél)
ability.aerialhell.spread_light.desc_3: Ettől egy ideig legyengülsz (egy rövid ideig, ha fel van szerelve teljes Hold Páncél).
ability.aerialhell.spread_light.desc_4: Passzív : +80% sebzés Árnylényeknek (Beleértve Megkötött Árnyékú játékosokat is).
ability.aerialhell.spread_light.cooldown: Hátralévő idő: 8s (4mp, ha fel van szerelve teljes Hold Páncél)
ability.aerialhell.lunar_tool.desc_2: Passzív : +40% sebzés Árnylényeknek (Beleértve Megkötött Árnyékú játékosokat is).
ability.aerialhell.volucite_power.desc_2: Lassú zuhanást biztosít egy rövid ideig.
ability.aerialhell.volucite_power.desc_3: További hatás, ha fel van szerelve teljes Volucit Páncél:
ability.aerialhell.glass_cannon_armored_: Ellenállás II és Lassúság hatást biztosít egy rövid ideig.
Felemelkedve! Felszállsz az égbe és megkapod a Lassú Zuhanás hatást.
ability.aerialhell.reaper_scythe.desc: Képesség találatkor: Tedd tönkre a célpontod életét, de a tiedet is...
ability.aerialhell.reaper_scythe.desc_2: Vakság, Gyengeség II, Lassúság II és Sérülékenység hatást okoz az ellenségnek, ha az nem egy Árnylény.
ability.aerialhell.reaper_scythe.desc_3: Sorvadás IV hatást okoz neked, ha az ellenség egy Árnylény.
ability.aerialhell.reaper_scythe.desc_4: Egy ellenség megütése elkerülhetetlen Sérülékenység hatást okoz neked.
ability.aerialhell.reaper_scythe.desc_5: Amíg van Sérülékenységed, az előző hatás nem fog ismét bekövetkezni.
ability.aerialhell.reaper_scythe.desc_6: Aktív: Láthatatlanságot, Gyorsaságot és Árny Immunitás hatást biztosít egy rövid ideig.
ability.aerialhell.reaper_scythe.conditi: Feltétele: Nincs felszerelve Hold Páncél. Hátralévő idő: 30mp
ability.aerialhell.magmatic_gel_tool.des: Mikor eltalálsz egy ellenséget, Lassúság hatást okoz a célpontnak.
(Lassúság II, ha fel van szerelve teljes Magmazselé Páncél).
ability.aerialhell.magmatic_gel_armor.de: Passzív: Mikor egy lény eltalál, Lassúság II hatást okoz neki.
ability.aerialhell.absolute_zero.desc_2: Passzív: Találatkor Lassúság III hatást okoz.
ability.aerialhell.absolute_zero.desc_3: Aktív: Lassúság IV hatást okoz minden közeli lénynek (Hátralévő idő: 15mp).
ability.aerialhell.arsonist_armor.desc_2: Passzív: -7% sebzést szenvedsz el (Páncélonként), mikor lángolsz.
ability.aerialhell.arsonist_armor.desc_3: Ha fel van szerelve teljes Arsonist Páncél, a teljes csökkenés -25%.
ability.aerialhell.arsonist_armor.desc_4: Mikor egy lény eltalál, lángba borítja.
ability.aerialhell.disadvantage_opponent: Mikor eltalálsz egy Lényt, Lassúság, Gyengeség és Fáradtság hatást okoz a célpontnak.
ability.aerialhell.cursed_tool.desc_4: - egy nem Árny- és Fénylényt: 2.0 kapsz, a célpontnak Sérülékenység hatást okoz.
ability.aerialhell.cursed_tool.desc_5: - egy Fénylényt: Sérülékenység II hatást okoz a célpontnak.
ability.aerialhell.magma_cube.desc_2: Passzív: Mikor a kezedben van, Magasugrás hatást biztosít.
Feltétele: A Gyorstárban, vagy a kezedben kell lennie.
ability.aerialhell.mine_stone_fast.desc_: Kétszer gyorsabban bányászod a Csillagoskövet, Csillagos Zúzottkövet, Árnykövet.
ability.aerialhell.forgotten_battle_trid: Delfin Kegyelme: Gyorsaság és Erő hatást biztosít egy rövid ideig.
ability.aerialhell.cursed_totem.desc_4: Más Biom: Gyengeség és Lassúság, hacsak nincs felszerelve teljes Árny Páncél.

aerialhell.guide_book.journey_1.journey_:
§oIn Aerial Hell, every island
§ocan lead your journey forward.
§oThe challenge is finding the right path.

This section acts like a simplified todo list for your progression in Aerial Hell.

It guides you through the main points of interest, but it will be up to you to discover the side objectives.
aerialhell.guide_book.journey_1.light_th:
You can find a broken abandonned portal up in the sky (on a cloud) in the overworld.
Repair it by moving the corners frame blocks to the holes, and light it with the Stellar Lighter.
aerialhell.guide_book.journey_2.step_2_c:
- Step 2: Collect basic resources
You can collect some wood from trees, mine some Stellar Stone and craft Stellar Cobblestone tools.
aerialhell.guide_book.journey_2.step_4_c:
- Step 4: Collect Fluorite
You will need Fluorite to make torches.
Fluorite is also a fuel.
aerialhell.guide_book.journey_2.cook_foo: You can cook some food if you want:
aerialhell.guide_book.journey_3.craft_os: For this, you will need Stellar Stone.
Then you can craft the Oscillator:
aerialhell.guide_book.journey_3.make_mat: First, oscillate your ores in Oscillator:
aerialhell.guide_book.journey_3.craft_ru:
Then, you can craft Ruby and Azurite equipment.
Ruby is the equivalent of Iron.
aerialhell.guide_book.journey_4.step_9_m:
- Step 9: Make a Farm
You will need more efficent food later. Take some time to make a farm.
aerialhell.guide_book.journey_4.breed_an: You can also breed animals to get more meat. Check the Bestiary section for more info about animals favorite food.
aerialhell.guide_book.journey_4.get_obsi:
To get some obsidian material.
Obsidian is the equivalent of diamond, but with more durability !
aerialhell.guide_book.journey_4.go_to_du: Check the Dungeon section for more info about dungeons.
aerialhell.guide_book.journey_5.step_11_:
- Step 11: Get an Enchanting Table
For this, you will need to craft obsidian:
aerialhell.guide_book.journey_5.get_leat: You will also need some leather:
aerialhell.guide_book.journey_5.improve_: To craft bookshelves and improve your enchanting table.
aerialhell.guide_book.journey_6.step_12_:
- Step 12: Enchant your stuff
Better equipment is required before rushing the next dungeon.

To get Lapis Lazuli in Aerial Hell, find and cut down some Lapis Robinia trees.
aerialhell.guide_book.journey_6.step_13_:
- Step 13: Craft a Ranged Weapon.
It will soon be essential !
aerialhell.guide_book.journey_6.lunar_te:
Making your way to the Lunar Priest boss can be difficult, but it is worth it: you will find valuable loot.
More info in the Dungeons section.
aerialhell.guide_book.journey_7.step_15_:
- Step 15: Prepare for Shadows
To explore the Shadow Biome with peace of mind, it is recommended to have the following items:
aerialhell.guide_book.journey_7.previous: Weapons of Light and Night Vision totem can be found in previous dungeons.
aerialhell.guide_book.journey_7.shadow_f: Shadow Fruits are looted by Shadow Pine Leaves.
When consumed, Shadow Fruit Stew grants Shadow Immunity.
aerialhell.guide_book.journey_7.shadow_c:
The catacombs are like a giant, multi-level maze, but it's well worth the effort.
Read the Dungeons section for more detailed info.
aerialhell.guide_book.journey_8.step_17_:
- Step 17: Get Volucite equipment
Now you have a Lunar Pickaxe, you can mine Volucite ore.
aerialhell.guide_book.journey_8.volucite:
You can also get Volucite by looting other Lunar Temples.
Volucite equipment is very powerful to explore and to fight what comes next.
aerialhell.guide_book.journey_8.golden_n: You will need to be well prepared to beat this dungeon. Prepare a lot of healing, an enchanted armor, and both ranged and melee weapons. Good luck !
aerialhell.guide_book.crafting_1.craftin:
§oEvery creation begins
§owith transformation.
§oSometimes, all it takes is
§oa single Oscillation.

This section guides you through the different types of crafting, their uses and their fuels.
Note that this section is not an exhaustive list of all recipes.
aerialhell.guide_book.crafting_1.type_1_:
- Smelting Recipe in Aerial Hell
As in the overworld, you can smelt ingredients and materials.
aerialhell.guide_book.crafting_1.get_sme: The main fuel for Smelting in Aerial Hell is Charcoal. You can obtain Charcoal by smelting any log in the furnace.
aerialhell.guide_book.crafting_2.smeltin: The main use for Smelting in Aerial Hell is cooking food.
aerialhell.guide_book.crafting_2.better_: But there is some other ways to improve the quality of your food.
aerialhell.guide_book.crafting_2.smeltin: Smelting ores is not recommended. It will make them overheat.
aerialhell.guide_book.crafting_2.transit:
Instead, you will have to oscillate your ores.

Note that if you overheated your ore, you can still oscillate it.
aerialhell.guide_book.crafting_3.type_2_:
- Oscillating Recipe
The main method for transforming your ingredients or materials. For this, you will need an Oscillator:
aerialhell.guide_book.crafting_3.oscilla: The main fuel for Oscillating is Fluorite, but you can also use Crystal.
Oscillating is the way to harness the potential of raw ores!

You can also oscillate
overheated ores.
aerialhell.guide_book.crafting_4.oscilla: Food can be improved through oscillation.
aerialhell.guide_book.crafting_4.other_t: But oscillating is not the only option for food.
aerialhell.guide_book.crafting_4.type_3_:
- Freezing Recipe
Another way to unlock potential.
aerialhell.guide_book.crafting_4.freezin:
The fuel for freezing is Magmatic gel.
There is some interesting recipes:
aerialhell.guide_book.crafting_5.frozen_: The special feature of frozen food is that it grants the Resistance effect.
aerialhell.guide_book.crafting_5.brewing:
- Brewing
Some Aerial Hell items also can be used as brewing ingredients:
aerialhell.guide_book.materials_1.materi:
§oKnowledge of materials
§ois the foundation of
§oevery skilled blacksmith.
This section contains a classification of Aerial Hell's materials, along with information about their unique properties.
Only the materials that can be used as fuel, or used to craft tools and armors will be presented in this section.

Materials tier list:

Sky Wood
(equivalent of overworld loot)
Fluorite (equivalent of coal)
Stellar Stone (equivalent of Stone)
Ruby (Equivalent of Iron)
Magmatic Gel
Azurite (equivalent of Gold)
Obsidian (equivalent of Diamond)
Lunar Crystal
(equivalent of Netherite)
Cursed Crystal
Volucite
Arsonist
aerialhell.guide_book.materials_2.fluori:
Commonly found as ore

Fuel for Oscillator

Fuel for
Light Reactors

Used in many crafts
aerialhell.guide_book.materials_2.ruby_d:
Commonly found as ore

Sometimes found in Structures

Must be put in Oscillation

Used in many crafts
aerialhell.guide_book.materials_3.magmat:
Commonly found as ore in bottom layer islands

Fuel for Freezer

Boots allows you to walk on Solid Ether Clouds

Slows down enemies

Used in many crafts
aerialhell.guide_book.materials_3.azurit:
Commonly found as ore

Fragile but efficient

Must be put in Oscillation

Used in some crafts
aerialhell.guide_book.materials_4.vanill:
- Overworld materials
Some can be found as ore in Aerial Hell:
Some can be found in structure loot chests:

Flint (in Abandonned Tower)
Redstone (in Abandonned Tower)
aerialhell.guide_book.materials_4.obsidi:
Found in Mud Dungeon

Rarely found as Ore

Allows to repair Mud Dungeon treasure loot

Used to craft
the Enchanting Table

Used to craft armor & tools
aerialhell.guide_book.materials_5.lunar_:
Only found in Lunar Temple

Increases your reach

Allows to repair Lunar Temple treasure loot

Used to craft armor & tools
aerialhell.guide_book.materials_5.cursed:
Found in Shadow Catacombs

Shadow armor granting
Shadow Bind power

Allows to repair Shadow Catacombs treasure loot

Used in many crafts
aerialhell.guide_book.materials_6.voluci:
Rarely found as Ore on top layer islands

Found in Lunar Temple

Special volucite power

Volucite ability

Used in many crafts
aerialhell.guide_book.materials_6.arsoni:
Found in Golden Nether Prison

Armor passive effects

Bonus when on fire

Allows to repair Golden Nether Prison treasure loot

Used in many crafts
aerialhell.guide_book.materials_7.smoky_:
Found as ore in Shadow biomes.
Can be used to replace Nether Quartz in crafting recipes, or for decoration.
aerialhell.guide_book.materials_7.crysta:
Found as Crystal Blocks in Crystal Biomes.
Can be used as fuel in Oscillator.
aerialhell.guide_book.materials_7.shadow:
Looted by some shadow creatures and in Shadow Catacombs chests.
Can be used as fuel in Shadow Reactors.
aerialhell.guide_book.effects_1.effects_:
§oWhether beneficial or harmful,
§oit is best to be prepared
§owhen receiving them
This section presents the specific effects of Aerial Hell and describes their properties.
aerialhell.guide_book.effects_2.head_in_: Allows you to hover in the air, and increases your fly speed
Allows you to walk on (White, Blue, Golden and Green) Solid Ether Clouds

Allows you to walk on
Ghost Blocks
Obtainable by consuming any Solid Ether Soups.
aerialhell.guide_book.effects_2.get_shad: Obtained by consuming a Shadow Fruit Stew
aerialhell.guide_book.effects_2.shadow_i: Grants you immunity against various negative effects of shadow biomes
Grants immunity to the Vulnerability effect, unless the source is Lilith
aerialhell.guide_book.effects_3.vulnerab: Each amplifier level of this effect doubles the damage taken.
If the damage is from Lilith boss, triples the damage instead.
aerialhell.guide_book.effects_3.get_vuln: Inflicted by some shadow creatures, including Lilith
aerialhell.guide_book.effects_3.get_shad: Obtained by wearing a full Shadow armor
aerialhell.guide_book.effects_3.shadow_b: Grants you immunity against various negative effects of shadow biomes
Makes you mislead Shadow creatures (they consider you as ally)
aerialhell.guide_book.effects_4.god_desc:
Regeneration-like effect (that can cummulate with other regeneration effects)

§oThis effect will be
§oreworked soon
aerialhell.guide_book.effects_4.traitor_:
Affected by this effect when you hit a misleaded (that consider you as ally) entity

Prevents you from misleading any entity
aerialhell.guide_book.enchantments_1.enc:
§oPower comes not
§oonly from crafting,
§obut also from enchanting.
This section presents the specific enchantments of Aerial Hell and describes their uses.
aerialhell.guide_book.enchantments_1.sol: Allows you to walk on (White, Blue, Golden and Green) Solid Ether Clouds

Allows you to walk on
Ghost Blocks
aerialhell.guide_book.enchantments_2.vul:
Treasure Enchantment

Found in Shadow Catacombs

If you hit a Light entity,
you will apply the Vulnerability effect to it.

Vulnerability significantly increases the damage taken by the affected entity.

For more info about Effects, refer to the Effects section.
aerialhell.guide_book.enchantments_2.lig:
Light entities are Lunatic Priest, Cortinarius Cow, Crystal Caterpillar, Crystal Golem, Crystal Slime, Crystal Spider and Golden Beech Mimic.

For more info about Entities, refer to the Bestiary section.
aerialhell.guide_book.environment_1.envi:
This section presents what you need to know about the environment to survive and progress. It outlines the specific properties (exclusive resources, dangers, etc.) of each biome category.
Note that this section is not an exhaustive list of all biomes.
aerialhell.guide_book.environment_1.aeri: Most classic biome of the dimension. Nothing special to note about this one. You can find some wood, materials and food.
Classic forest biome. There is a dangerous creature in it, the Flying Jellyfish:
aerialhell.guide_book.environment_2.lapi: Blue trees savana biome. This is a very useful biome because you can find Lapis Robinia trees in it, which allows you to obtain lapis lazuli.
aerialhell.guide_book.environment_2.copp:
Copper pine forest
Copper pine highland forest
Common biome in which you can find big pines, copper pine cone, and unique creatures like the Kodama.
aerialhell.guide_book.environment_2.stel: Jungle biome with gigantic trees. Unique vegetation and creatures.
aerialhell.guide_book.environment_3.shro:
Giant red mushroom forest
Verdigris agaric forest
Cortinarius violaceus forest
aerialhell.guide_book.environment_3.crys:
Biomes populated by crystal clusters and crystal creatures.
Shadow biome can't spread in those biomes.
aerialhell.guide_book.environment_4.dese: Desert biomes with sparse vegetation and dangerous creatures, but interesting structures, like the Upside down Pyramid.
aerialhell.guide_book.environment_4.shad: Hostile biome with spreading around the world. Dangerous vegetation and creatures. Worth it structures and loots.
aerialhell.guide_book.bestiary_1.bestiar:
§oTo survive among creatures,
§olearn them before
§oyou challenge them.
This section presents the different types of creatures you can find in Aerial Hell

Entities are sorted by type:
- Friendly animals
- Neutral creatures
- Hostile enemies

Hostile enemies include the creatures you can find in structures.

The bosses aren't presented in this section. If you want to learn more about bosses, please refer to the "Bosses" section.
aerialhell.guide_book.bestiary_2.friendl:
- Friendly Animals

They don't attack even if provoked.
 They are breedable and their preferred food is shown next to the "Breedable" designation.
aerialhell.guide_book.bestiary_2.gliding:
Common animal
Drops food
Breedable
Jumps and glides!
aerialhell.guide_book.bestiary_2.stellar:
Common animal
Drops food
Lays eggs
Breedable
aerialhell.guide_book.bestiary_2.sandy_s:
Common animal
Drops food
Breedable
Its wool falls off when hit
aerialhell.guide_book.bestiary_2.friendl: Previous animals can be found in all biomes except shadow and desert biomes
aerialhell.guide_book.bestiary_2.kodama:
- Kodama
Only found in Stellar Jungle
Disappears during day time or if it feels threatened
Breedable
aerialhell.guide_book.bestiary_3.neutral:
- Neutral Creatures

They don't attack unless provoked.
aerialhell.guide_book.bestiary_3.fat_pha:
Common flying creature
Drops food
Hits hard early-game
Disappears on moonlight
aerialhell.guide_book.bestiary_3.hostile:
- Hostile Ennemies

They appear naturally. They are sorted by type ("normal", shroom, crystal, etc.)
aerialhell.guide_book.bestiary_3.flying_:
Only in Forest biome
Shoots poisonballs
aerialhell.guide_book.bestiary_4.cortina:
Found in Shroom biomes
Drops rotten leather
aerialhell.guide_book.bestiary_4.shroomb:
Found in Shroom biomes
Drops gunpowder
aerialhell.guide_book.bestiary_5.venomou:
Found in Desert biomes
Poison its target
aerialhell.guide_book.bestiary_5.shadow_:
Appears in Shadow biomes
Drops shadow shard

Found in
Shadow Catacombs
Dangerous encounter
aerialhell.guide_book.bestiary_5.mud_sol:
Found in Mud Dungeon
Drops Mud Bone
aerialhell.guide_book.bestiary_6.element:
- Elemental Spirit
Comes in 3 different variants. When they get close enough to you, they disappear by doing the following action:

Sets you on fire


Slows you down


Summons a lightning
aerialhell.guide_book.bestiary_6.torn_sp:
Found in Golden Nether Prison
Can shoot
aerialhell.guide_book.bestiary_6.slime_p:
Found in Floating Boat
Has Ninja variant
aerialhell.guide_book.bestiary_6.ghost_s:
Found in Ghost Floating Boat
Has Ninja variant
aerialhell.guide_book.bosses_1.bosses_se:
§oThey will either mark the
§onext step of your journey...
§oor its end.
aerialhell.guide_book.bosses_1.bosses_co:
This section provides detailed information about each boss found in Aerial Hell, including their abilities and combat phases.

It does not cover the recommended equipment or progression required to defeat them. For that information, refer to the Journey section.
aerialhell.guide_book.bosses_1.boss_list:
- Mud Cycle Mage



- Lunar Priest


- Lilith



- Chained God
aerialhell.guide_book.bosses_2.mud_cycle:
Found in Mud Dungeon, this boss has the ability to summon increasingly powerful allies during the fight.

The time before the next summoning decreases depending on the damage taken.
aerialhell.guide_book.bosses_2.mage_base: Among the entities that the Mage can summon are:
aerialhell.guide_book.bosses_2.mage_dupl: If the Mage feels threatened by too many enemies at once, he can summon copies of himself to confuse enemies.
aerialhell.guide_book.bosses_3.lunar_pri:
Found in Lunar Temple, this boss has two phases and the ability to throw Lunar Projectiles at enemies.

Its behavior changes significantly during the second phase.
aerialhell.guide_book.bosses_3.first_pha:
First Phase:
The Priest will fly randomly like a ghast while throwing Lunar Projectiles to his enemies.
aerialhell.guide_book.bosses_3.second_ph:
Second Phase:
Starts when the Priest's life drops below half.
The Lunar Priest begins to walk instead of flying, tries to melee attack his enemies, while still throwing Lunar Projectiles at a faster pace.
aerialhell.guide_book.bosses_4.transform: Found in the Shadow Catacombs, Lilith awaits enemies with an angelic appearance. But do not be fooled. Her true form will soon emerge, altering both her appearance and the room around her.
aerialhell.guide_book.bosses_4.lilith_at:
Lilith melee attacks her enemies. When she strikes, she inflicts the Vulnerability effect unless you have Shadow Immunity.
Check the Effects section for more info about effect properties.
aerialhell.guide_book.bosses_4.lilith_ab:
In addition to melee attacks, Lilith has two abilities that are triggered when she is approximately mid-life.

The exact threshold varies depending on the number of nearby enemies: the more enemies present, the earlier these abilities are triggered.
aerialhell.guide_book.bosses_5.lilith_su: The first one is the ability to summon Shadow Flying Skulls, which will attack you in the same way as the Vex.
aerialhell.guide_book.bosses_5.lilith_sh: Its other ability is to throw Shadow Projectiles. Shadow projectiles inflict Vulnerability, even with Shadow Immunity.
aerialhell.guide_book.bosses_5.chained_g:
When entering his room in the Golden Nether Prison, the Chained God will be passive because he is chained.

If any enemy approach him or if he is hurt, he will unchain himself with a roar, throwing fireballs, and start fighting.
aerialhell.guide_book.bosses_6.chained_g: In his first phase, the Chained God attacks his targets in melee combat. He inflicts massive damage and knockback. He also throws some fireballs at targets once he is mid-life. It is possible to attack him with ranged weapons during this phase.
After reaching a certain amount of remaining health, he will enter its second phase with a powerful roar that will summon several Torn Spirits.
During the second phase, the Chained God is immune to ranged damage, and uses a new ability.
He begins to "implode" regularly. When he does, he raises his arms, pulling you towards him.. and after a short time, it implodes, causing a lot of damage.
aerialhell.guide_book.structures_1.struc:
§oEvery structure hides a secret.
§oThe challenge is reaching it alive.

This section lists all the structures related to Aerial Hell.
Only the most essential ones are detailed and illustrated here.

Dungeons are covered separately in the "Dungeons" section.
aerialhell.guide_book.structures_1.aband:
Broken portals can be found in both the Overworld and Aerial Hell.
In the Overworld, they appear on clouds high in the sky.
In Aerial Hell, they can appear either on clouds or on the ground.
aerialhell.guide_book.structures_2.float:
Giant boats flying in the air. They are filled with Ruby treasure and Slime Pirates.
Exists in two classic variants: Aerial Tree and Lapis Robinia
aerialhell.guide_book.structures_2.ghost: This variant is slightly different. To access it and interact with the Azurite treasures and Ghost Pirates, you will either need to wear Magmatic Gel boots or have the "Head in the Clouds" effect
aerialhell.guide_book.structures_3.stell:
An abandonned tower you will frequently encounter during your journey.
It contains interesting treasures to get started, and a few creatures.
aerialhell.guide_book.structures_3.upsid: Found in desert biomes, this Pyramid is teeming with treasures and hostile creatures.
aerialhell.guide_book.structures_4.littl:
Aerial Hell also contains many smaller structures, including:

- Copper pine cottage
- Lapis Robinia hut
- Shadow Pine Tower
- Desert abandonned structure

These locations often contain useful loots, and can provide shelter during your journey.
Here is an example of a small structure: the Shadow Pine Tower, found in Shadow biomes.
aerialhell.guide_book.dungeons_1.dungeon:
§oEvery dungeon stands between you
§oand the next step of your journey.
This section presents the dungeons found in Aerial Hell. It provides an overview of how to explore them and indicates the bosses that can be found within.

Mud Dungeon

Lunar Temple

Shadow Catacombs

Golden Nether Prison
To be able to destroy dungeon blocks and access treasure chests, you must destroy the Dungeon Core. Each dungeon has its own Core.
aerialhell.guide_book.dungeons_2.mud_dun:
This dungeon is located in the lower islands.

It is carved into the islands and consists of corridors and old, abandoned rooms.

If you venture deep enough into the dungeon, you will eventually come across a room guarded by two Mud Golems. The corridor defended by these two golems will lead you to the boss room, where the Mud Cycle Mage awaits.
Once you defeated the Mud Cycle Mage, remember to destroy the Core to be able to access the treasures and destroy the blocks.
aerialhell.guide_book.dungeons_3.lunar_t: This giant temple located in the air, at the upper islands level, has a single entrance on one of its sides.
Once inside, you will need to make your way upward through the floors. Explore each level and find the room that leads to the next.

Once you reach the top, you will notice a staircase leading down to the boss room, where the Lunar Priest awaits.
The Dungeon Core is located in the center of the boss room, almost at ground level, near the treasure chests.
aerialhell.guide_book.dungeons_4.shadow_: Located in Shadow Biomes, this dungeon spans three levels: a ground floor and two basements. Several entrances provide access to it from the ground floor.

Once inside, your goal is to reach the second basement. Begin by taking the staircase at the center of the ground floor, then navigate the maze-like first basement to locate the passage leading deeper below.
Watch out for the Shadow Trolls guarding Lilith's room. You know the drill; remember to destroy the Core, which is in the center of the room, in the ground.
aerialhell.guide_book.dungeons_5.golden_: This dungeon is hidden within an ellipsoidal floating island topped with a few small structures. It is located between the two layers of islands.
You will need to explore the prison to find the entrance leading to the Chained God's chamber. The chamber contains some Liquid of Gods, which is dangerous, and spawners.
Defeat the Chained God, then destroy the Core in the center of the room, in the ground, to be able to access the treasure chests and destroy the blocks.
aerialhell.guide_book.shadow_and_light_1:
§oEvery soul harbors both
§oShadow and Light.
§oThe question is which one will prevail.

This section introduces the two factions of Aerial Hell.

Locked in an ongoing war, each faction seeks to defeat the other.

Here, you will learn how to earn a faction's trust and assist them in their struggle against their rival.

- Shadow and Light War

The lands of Aerial Hell are shaped by an endless conflict between Light and Shadow. While their goals differ, neither side is inherently good or evil. The choice of whom to support is yours.
aerialhell.guide_book.shadow_and_light_2:
- Light

The Light seeks to preserve life as it is known throughout Aerial Hell.

Crystal biomes, Light creatures, and thriving ecosystems all flourish under its influence. Wherever the Light prevails, nature remains vibrant and familiar.

The forces of Light constantly oppose the spread of Shadow, protecting the lands they consider worth preserving.

Many creatures aligned with the Light can be found near Crystal biomes and Lunar structures, where their influence is strongest.
aerialhell.guide_book.shadow_and_light_3:
- Shadow

Though its lands may appear dark and desolate, they possess their own ecosystems, vegetation, and creatures. Life exists there in forms unfamiliar to most travelers.
Some hostile creatures are considered Shadow creatures but can be present even outside of Shadow biomes, such as Enderman or Evil Cow.

The Shadow seeks to expand its influence across Aerial Hell, transforming the world so that its own forms of life may thrive.

Most of Shadow Creatures are found in Shadow Biomes, but some are exclusive to Shadow Catacombs.
aerialhell.guide_book.shadow_and_light_4:
Do not spread naturally
Some blocks naturally protect from Shadows
Light Projectile repels Shadow
You can use Light Reactors to protect areas from Shadow or to uncorrupt:

Spreads naturally
Can't spread in Crystal biomes
Shadow Projectile spreads Shadow
You can place any corrupted block to start a new spread, or use Shadow Reactors to corrupt an area:
aerialhell.guide_book.shadow_and_light_5: By wearing a full set of Lunar Armor, you can disguise yourself as an ally of Light. Light creatures will no longer attack you.
Supporting one faction does not prevent you from interacting with the other. However, understanding their differences is essential if you wish to influence the balance of power in Aerial Hell.
By wearing a full set of Shadow Armor, you can disguise yourself as an ally of Shadow. Shadow creatures will no longer attack you.
The Shadow Armor will completely change your perspective on Shadow. What better way to understand Shadow than to become a Shadow?
aerialhell.guide_book.items_1.items_sect:
§oNo item is legendary by itself.
§oOnly the actions it enables
§ocan make it so.
aerialhell.guide_book.items_1.items_cont: This section is not an exhaustive list of items. It only presents those whose usefulness may not be immediately obvious.
aerialhell.guide_book.items_1.magmatic_g:
- Magmatic Gel Boots
Allows you to walk on solid ether clouds and ghostly blocks. Essential for traveling across the skies and exploring Ghost Floating Boats.
aerialhell.guide_book.items_1.ability_it:
- Ability Items
Many items in Aerial Hell possess unique abilities. You can read an item's tooltip to learn more about its properties.
Passive Abilities are always active and require no action from you.
Active Abilities can be triggered by right-clicking while holding the item.
aerialhell.guide_book.items_2.volucite_t:
- Volucite Torch
When placed, it frightens Shadow Automatons and Shadow Trolls, making them run away.
aerialhell.guide_book.items_2.ruby_aeria:
- Ruby Aerial Berry
When consumed, provides a 2-minute Health Boost
aerialhell.guide_book.items_2.volucite_a:
- Volucite Aerial Berry
When consumed, provides a 4-minute Head in the Clouds effect
aerialhell.guide_book.items_2.azurite_co:
- Azurite Copper Pine Cone
When consumed, provides a 20-second Haste effect
aerialhell.guide_book.items_2.gods_voluc:
- Gods Volucite Aerial Berry
When consumed, provides a variety of positive effects:
Slow Falling III, Head in the Clouds III, Shadow Immunity III, Resistance I, Strength II, Haste II, Absorption IV for approximately 2 minutes, God and Fire Resistance for 5 minutes.
